# Network Performance & Rendering Performance (Senior Frontend Interview)

These are two of the most frequently asked performance topics for **Senior React / Frontend Engineer** interviews.

***

# Part 1: Network Performance

Network performance focuses on:

```text
How fast resources reach the browser
```

Resources:

```text
HTML
CSS
JS
Images
Fonts
API Responses
Videos
```

***

# Browser Request Lifecycle

```text
User enters URL
        │
        ▼
DNS Lookup
        ▼
TCP Handshake
        ▼
SSL/TLS
        ▼
Request Sent
        ▼
TTFB
        ▼
Content Download
        ▼
Browser Process
```

Every HTTP request incurs networking overhead beyond simply downloading the file. [\[dev.to\]](https://dev.to/woai3c/24-front-end-performance-optimization-tips-4a6c)

***

# Key Network Metrics

## DNS Lookup

```text
Domain → IP
```

Example:

```text
google.com
↓
142.x.x.x
```

***

## TTFB (Time To First Byte)

Measures:

```text
Request
 ↓
Server Response Starts
```

A high TTFB often indicates backend, origin, or network issues. [\[dev.to\]](https://dev.to/woai3c/24-front-end-performance-optimization-tips-4a6c)

***

## Download Time

Measures:

```text
Server Response
↓
Browser Receives File
```

Affected by:

```text
File Size
Compression
Bandwidth
```

***

# Network Optimisation Techniques

***

## 1. Reduce Requests

Bad:

```text
20 CSS Files
30 JS Files
```

Good:

```text
Bundled Assets
```

Reducing the number of requests can improve loading performance. [\[dev.to\]](https://dev.to/woai3c/24-front-end-performance-optimization-tips-4a6c), [\[dev.to\]](https://dev.to/tianyaschool/in-depth-understanding-of-front-end-performance-optimization-from-network-to-rendering-kfj)

***

## 2. HTTP/2

HTTP/2 supports:

```text
Multiplexing
```

Instead of:

```text
Request 1
Wait

Request 2
Wait
```

You get:

```text
Request 1
Request 2
Request 3
Parallel Streams
```

HTTP/2 reduces request blocking and increases loading speed. [\[dev.to\]](https://dev.to/tianyaschool/in-depth-understanding-of-front-end-performance-optimization-from-network-to-rendering-kfj), [\[linkedin.com\]](https://www.linkedin.com/pulse/in-depth-understanding-front-end-performance-network-rendering-zeng-sfmvc)

***

## 3. Compression

Enable:

```text
Gzip

Brotli
```

Example:

```text
500 KB JS
↓
120 KB
```

Compressing text resources reduces transmission volume. [\[dev.to\]](https://dev.to/tianyaschool/in-depth-understanding-of-front-end-performance-optimization-from-network-to-rendering-kfj), [\[linkedin.com\]](https://www.linkedin.com/pulse/in-depth-understanding-front-end-performance-network-rendering-zeng-sfmvc)

***

## 4. Caching

### Browser Cache

```http
Cache-Control:
public,max-age=31536000
```

### CDN Cache

```text
Cloudflare
CloudFront
Fastly
```

Caching reduces repeat downloads and latency. [\[devstudioit.com\]](https://devstudioit.com/en/blog/advanced-performance-optimization-cdn-caching-2026)

***

## 5. CDN

Without CDN:

```text
India → US Server
```

With CDN:

```text
India → Nearby Edge
```

Benefits:

```text
Lower latency
Faster delivery
Better LCP
```

CDNs serve content from geographically distributed servers closer to users. [\[devstudioit.com\]](https://devstudioit.com/en/blog/advanced-performance-optimization-cdn-caching-2026), [\[ortemtech.com\]](https://ortemtech.com/blog/frontend-performance-optimization/)

***

## 6. Preload Critical Resources

```![Visualization](data:image/png;base64,)

Loads critical resources earlier. [\[dev.to\]](https://dev.to/tianyaschool/in-depth-understanding-of-front-end-performance-optimization-from-network-to-rendering-kfj), [\[linkedin.com\]](https://www.linkedin.com/pulse/in-depth-understanding-front-end-performance-network-rendering-zeng-sfmvc)

***

## 7. Prefetch Future Resources

```![Visualization](data:image/png;base64,)

Used for:

```text
Next Page
Future Routes
```

[\[dev.to\]](https://dev.to/tianyaschool/in-depth-understanding-of-front-end-performance-optimization-from-network-to-rendering-kfj), [\[linkedin.com\]](https://www.linkedin.com/pulse/in-depth-understanding-front-end-performance-network-rendering-zeng-sfmvc)

***

## 8. Code Splitting

Bad:

```text
bundle.js
3 MB
```

Good:

```text
home.chunk.js

dashboard.chunk.js

settings.chunk.js
```

Dynamic imports reduce initial loading time. [\[dev.to\]](https://dev.to/tianyaschool/in-depth-understanding-of-front-end-performance-optimization-from-network-to-rendering-kfj), [\[linkedin.com\]](https://www.linkedin.com/pulse/in-depth-understanding-front-end-performance-network-rendering-zeng-sfmvc)

```js
const Dashboard =
  React.lazy(() =>
    import("./Dashboard")
  );
```

***

# Network Performance Interview Answer

> I optimise network performance by reducing requests, enabling HTTP/2, compressing responses, using aggressive caching, serving assets via CDN, preloading critical resources, prefetching future routes, and splitting JavaScript bundles to minimise initial page load. [\[dev.to\]](https://dev.to/tianyaschool/in-depth-understanding-of-front-end-performance-optimization-from-network-to-rendering-kfj), [\[devstudioit.com\]](https://devstudioit.com/en/blog/advanced-performance-optimization-cdn-caching-2026)

***

# Part 2: Rendering Performance

Rendering performance focuses on:

```text
How quickly the browser
converts code into pixels.
```

***

# Browser Rendering Pipeline

```text
HTML
 ↓
DOM

CSS
 ↓
CSSOM

DOM + CSSOM
 ↓
Render Tree

Layout
 ↓
Paint
 ↓
Composite
```

Rendering optimisation focuses on keeping this pipeline efficient. [\[dev.to\]](https://dev.to/tianyaschool/in-depth-understanding-of-front-end-performance-optimization-from-network-to-rendering-kfj), [\[linkedin.com\]](https://www.linkedin.com/pulse/in-depth-understanding-front-end-performance-network-rendering-zeng-sfmvc)

***

# Layout (Reflow)

Occurs when geometry changes.

Example:

```js
element.style.width =
  "300px";
```

Browser recalculates:

```text
Width
Height
Position
```

***

# Paint (Repaint)

Occurs when appearance changes.

```js
element.style.color =
  "red";
```

Browser redraws pixels.

No layout calculation needed.

***

# Reflow vs Repaint

| Operation | Cost      |
| --------- | --------- |
| Repaint   | Medium    |
| Reflow    | Expensive |

Reflows are generally more costly because they require layout recalculation.

***

# Layout Thrashing

Bad:

```js
for(let i = 0; i < 1000; i++) {
  element.style.width =
    i + "px";

  element.offsetWidth;
}
```

Every loop:

```text
Write
Read
Write
Read
```

Forces repeated layout calculation.

***

# Better

```js
requestAnimationFrame(() => {
  element.style.width =
    "500px";
});
```

Using `requestAnimationFrame` is commonly recommended for smoother visual updates. [\[dev.to\]](https://dev.to/tianyaschool/in-depth-understanding-of-front-end-performance-optimization-from-network-to-rendering-kfj), [\[linkedin.com\]](https://www.linkedin.com/pulse/in-depth-understanding-front-end-performance-network-rendering-zeng-sfmvc)

***

# Expensive CSS Properties

Bad:

```css
top
left
width
height
```

Cause layout updates.

***

# Preferred Animation Properties

```css
transform
opacity
```

Example:

```css
.card {
  transition:
    transform .3s ease;
}

.card:hover {
  transform:
    translateY(-10px);
}
```

Transform and opacity animations are generally preferred because they avoid triggering layout recalculations.

***

# React Rendering Performance

***

## React.memo

```jsx
const Product =
 React.memo(
   ProductCard
 );
```

Avoids unnecessary component renders.

***

## useMemo

```jsx
const filteredProducts =
 useMemo(() => {
   return products.filter(
     filterFn
   );
 }, [products]);
```

Caches expensive calculations.

***

## useCallback

```jsx
const onClick =
 useCallback(() => {
 }, []);
```

Prevents function recreation.

***

## Virtualisation

Bad:

```text
10000 Rows
```

Good:

```jsx
react-window
```

Only visible rows render.

***

# Critical Rendering Path Optimisation

### Inline Critical CSS

&lt;style&gt;.hero { color:red;}&lt;/style&gt;![Visualization](data:image/png;base64,)

This helps avoid render-blocking for above-the-fold content. [\[dev.to\]](https://dev.to/tianyaschool/in-depth-understanding-of-front-end-performance-optimization-from-network-to-rendering-kfj), [\[linkedin.com\]](https://www.linkedin.com/pulse/in-depth-understanding-front-end-performance-network-rendering-zeng-sfmvc)

***

### Defer JS

app.jsscript&gt;![Visualization](data:image/png;base64,)

Prevents JavaScript from blocking rendering. [\[dev.to\]](https://dev.to/tianyaschool/in-depth-understanding-of-front-end-performance-optimization-from-network-to-rendering-kfj), [\[linkedin.com\]](https://www.linkedin.com/pulse/in-depth-understanding-front-end-performance-network-rendering-zeng-sfmvc)

***

### Lazy Load Images

&lt;img loading="lazy"/&gt;![Visualization](data:image/png;base64,)

Loads only when needed. [\[dev.to\]](https://dev.to/tianyaschool/in-depth-understanding-of-front-end-performance-optimization-from-network-to-rendering-kfj)

***

# Senior Frontend 2-Minute Interview Answer

> Network performance is about delivering resources efficiently using HTTP/2, compression, caching, CDNs, preload/prefetch strategies, and code splitting. Rendering performance is about minimising work in the browser rendering pipeline by avoiding excessive reflows, reducing repaints, animating with transform and opacity, virtualising large lists, and preventing unnecessary React re-renders using React.memo, useMemo, and useCallback. Together these optimisations improve Core Web Vitals, responsiveness, and overall user experience. [\[dev.to\]](https://dev.to/tianyaschool/in-depth-understanding-of-front-end-performance-optimization-from-network-to-rendering-kfj), [\[ortemtech.com\]](https://ortemtech.com/blog/frontend-performance-optimization/), [\[devstudioit.com\]](https://devstudioit.com/en/blog/advanced-performance-optimization-cdn-caching-2026)
